{{Infobox character
| name = Lyathatan
| appears_in = The Elder Empire: Sea
| titles = Elderspawn, Elder, bound Elder
| abilities = Reading
| affiliations = Kelarac
| introduced = Of Sea and Shadow
}}

''See also: [[Lyathatan Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The Lyathatan is an ancient and massive entity known as an [[Elderspawn]]. Originating from [[Kelarac]], this creature possesses a physical form that combines shark-like and nightmare-inducing features, including blue-green skin, spines on its back, a fin, and six black eyes. Its anatomy includes clawed, six-fingered hands, tentacles, and a lower jaw filled with shark-like teeth. It also possesses legs that resemble a hybrid of human limbs and fish tails. The Lyathatan is capable of immense feats of strength, such as lifting ships out of the water or engaging in combat with other massive beings like a kraken [[Elder]]. Despite its terrifying appearance and capacity for destruction, the Lyathatan is currently bound to a human named [[Calder]] through a contract or leash. While it experiences complex, alien emotions—ranging from intense rage and contempt for lesser creatures to calculated patience and subtle amusement—it obeys [[Calder's Intent]]. This obedience is often driven by the creature's own long-term, intricate plans that span centuries, making its current servitude a mere step in a much larger cosmic game. The Lyathatan is highly resilient, able to go long periods without food, and possesses a consciousness characterized by profound age and incomprehensible goals.}}

== Of Sea and Shadow ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|Hailing from [[Kelarac]], the Lyathatan is an ancient [[Elderspawn]] of vast hunger and indescribable age that exists in servitude to [[Calder]]. This massive, man-shaped creature possesses fish-like scales, a ridged spine, gills, and three black eyes on either side of its head. As an entity with a deep and fathomless mind, it can be commanded by [[Calder]], who uses its awareness to read information such as the shape of an [[island]]. The Lyathatan functions as a ship that can anchor in the [[Aion]] and is capable of pulling vessels like [[The Testament]] using its chains. Despite their link, [[Calder]] can only barely feel the creature and requires enhancements from [[Kelarac]] to communicate more effectively with it.}}

{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The [[Lyathatan]] is a giant creature that pulls [[Calder's ship]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c3">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The [[Lyathatan]] is a ship that can anchor in the [[Aion]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c5">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The [[Lyathatan]] is a ship or entity [[Calder]] can command.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c5" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The [[Lyathatan]] is the ship [[Calder]] is on.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c7">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The [[Lyathatan]] is a ship with chains and an awareness that can be read.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Calder]] [[Read]] from the [[Lyathatan]] to discover the [[island]]'s shape.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c9">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Lyathatan]] are creatures that live alongside [[Calder]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The [[Lyathatan]] hails from [[Kelarac]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c11">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The [[Lyathatan]] is a creature or entity that could match the [[Children of the Dead Mother]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c12">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The [[Lyathatan]] is drifting beneath the [[island]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c12" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The [[Lyathatan]] is a location or entity with a shadow that writhes beneath the water.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c16">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Calder]] mentions feeding themselves to the [[Lyathatan]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The [[Lyathatan]] has blue, six-fingered, webbed hands.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c18">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|A [[Lyathatan]] is something people can have a link to.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c22">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Calder]] left orders for the [[Lyathatan]] to take his ship around to the back of the [[island]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c23">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Calder Marten]] can barely feel the [[Lyathatan]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c23" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The [[Lyathatan]] is a huge creature shaped like a man that pulls the ship [[The Testament]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c24">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The [[Lyathatan]] has fish-like scales and a spine with ridges.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The [[Lyathatan]] has six-fingered hands that are webbed and tipped with spike-like nails.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The [[Lyathatan]] has a shark's mouth and three black eyes on either side of its head.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The [[Lyathatan]] has gills on its short, muscular neck.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The [[Lyathatan]] is an [[Elderspawn]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The [[Lyathatan]] is a being of indescribable age and vast hunger.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The [[Lyathatan]] is in servitude to [[Calder]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|A deeper link to the [[Lyathatan]] is required for help.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c25">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The [[Lyathatan]] has a hand with six clawed, double-jointed fingers.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c25" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The [[Lyathatan]] has a deep and fathomless mind.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c25" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The [[Lyathatan]] is a being with passion in its expression.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c25" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|A [[Lyathatan]] can be used to pull a ship.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c26">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 26</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Kelarac]] enhanced [[Calder]]'s ability to communicate with the [[Lyathatan]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c27">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 27</ref>}}

== Of Dawn and Darkness ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|An [[Elder-spawned]] echo of a mermaid, the Lyathatan is a vessel that drifts sluggishly beneath [[The Testament]]. This creature possesses deep blue scales, six black eyes arranged in two rows of three, a shark-like mouth with jagged teeth, and a man's torso with muscular, taloned arms. As a bound [[Elder]] sworn into service by invested chains, the Lyathatan serves [[Kelarac]] and is a being to whom [[Calder]] sent his [[Intent]]. [[Calder]] uses [[Reading]] to communicate with the entity, which appears as if it were constructed from nightmares and sharks. While [[Calder]] originally intended for the Lyathatan to handle a whale-sized shadow, it remains a vessel that can slow to a crawl and might be persuaded to move by a [[Reader]].}}

{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The [[Lyathatan]] has deep blue scales, six black eyes in two rows of three, and a shark-like mouth with jagged teeth.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c2">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The [[Lyathatan]] has a torso like a man's and muscular arms with taloned hands.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The [[Lyathatan]] is described as an [[Elder-spawned]] echo of a mermaid.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The [[Lyathatan]] is an [[Elderspawn]] with six black eyes.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The voice asked if they could capture the [[Lyathatan]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c3">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The [[Lyathatan]] is a being that [[Calder]] sent his [[Intent]] down to.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c4">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The appearance of the [[Lyathatan]] delighted [[Jerri]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c5">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The [[Lyathatan]] is a bound [[Elder]] sworn into service by invested chains.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c5" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Calder]] uses [[Reading]] to communicate with the [[Lyathatan]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c6">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The [[Lyathatan]] is an [[Elder]] that [[Calder]] can [[Read]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c11">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The [[Lyathatan]] is a creature that looks like it is made of sharks and nightmares.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c13">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Calder]] originally planned to let the [[Lyathatan]] deal with the whale-sized shadow.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c19">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The [[Lyathatan]] is a ship that drifts sluggishly beneath [[The Testament]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c20">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|A [[Reader]] might be able to persuade the [[Lyathatan]] to move.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c21">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The [[Lyathatan]] is a servant of [[Kelarac]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c23">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The [[Lyathatan]] is a vessel that can slow to a crawl.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c23" />}}

== Of Kings and Killers ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The Lyathatan is an [[Elder]] creature with six black eyes that emerged from the [[Aion Sea]]. Though it once rested on the sea floor, this entity is actually an [[Elder]] bound to the hull of [[Calder's ship]] by chains and manacles. While it previously cleared the waters surrounding the vessel per an agreement, the creature had been shirking its duties and caused enough fear to necessitate heavy guards for the Testament. [[Calder]] maintains the ability to issue mental orders to the creature, which was observed wading through the ocean while dragging a ship and snarling as it cupped its missing hand.}}

{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The [[Lyathatan]] is a ship that rests on the sea floor.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c4">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The [[Lyathatan]] cleared the waters around the ship per its agreement.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c14">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The [[Lyathatan]] is a ship.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c16">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The [[Lyathatan]] has manacles.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[The Testament]] is under heavy guard due to fear of the [[Lyathatan]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c19">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The [[Lyathatan]] is an [[Elder]] creature bound to the hull of [[Calder's ship]] by chains.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c20">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The [[Lyathatan]] is wading through the ocean and dragging a ship.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c22">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The [[Lyathatan]] is an [[Elder]] that [[Calder]] can mentally order.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c24">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The [[Lyathatan]] is a creature that was shirking its duties.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The [[Lyathatan]] is a creature with six black eyes that burst from the [[Aion Sea]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c25">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The [[Lyathatan]] was snarling and cupping its missing hand.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c25" />}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|Based on what he Read from the Lyathatan, Calder discovered that the “island” actually curved up on the bottom.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c9" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|“Either way, you need a deeper link to the Lyathatan.”<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c25" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|“Can you capture the Lyathatan?” the voice asked, creaking like the mast of a ship in high wind.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c3" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Elder Empire nav}}

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:Of Sea and Shadow]]
[[Category:Of Dawn and Darkness]]
[[Category:Of Kings and Killers]]
[[Category:Elder Empire]]
[[Category:Elder Empire characters]]
[[Category:Titled characters]]
[[Category:Readers]]
